questions.question: In which city is the Taj Mahal located?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is located in Agra, Uttar Pradesh. It was built by Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal.
questions.funFact: The Taj Mahal changes colour throughout the day — pinkish in the morning, white in the afternoon, and golden at night!

questions.question: What colour is the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is made of white marble. The marble was brought from Rajasthan and took over 20 years to build.
questions.funFact: Around 1,000 elephants were used to carry the heavy marble stones to build the Taj Mahal!

questions.question: The Red Fort is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort is located in Old Delhi. It was the main residence of the Mughal emperors for nearly 200 years.
questions.funFact: Every year on Independence Day (15th August), the Prime Minister of India hoists the national flag at the Red Fort!

questions.question: Why is the Red Fort called 'Red'?
options: It is made of red sandstone
questions.explanation: The Red Fort is called 'Red' because it is built using red sandstone. The walls of the fort are made entirely of this beautiful red stone.
questions.funFact: The Red Fort is so big that it has its own market inside called Chhatta Chowk, where people used to shop hundreds of years ago!

questions.question: The Gateway of India is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Gateway of India is located in Mumbai, Maharashtra. It stands on the waterfront at Apollo Bunder, facing the Arabian Sea.
questions.funFact: The Gateway of India was built to welcome King George V and Queen Mary when they visited India in 1911!

questions.question: India Gate is a famous monument in which city?
questions.explanation: India Gate is located in New Delhi. It is a war memorial built to honour the Indian soldiers who died in World War I.
questions.funFact: India Gate is 42 metres tall — that is taller than a 13-storey building!

questions.question: What is the Qutub Minar famous for being?
options: The tallest brick minaret in the world
questions.explanation: The Qutub Minar in Delhi is the tallest brick minaret in the world at 72.5 metres. It was started by Qutub-ud-din Aibak in 1193.
questions.funFact: The Qutub Minar is slightly tilted, just like the Leaning Tower of Pisa in Italy!

questions.question: Which monument looks like a giant archway near the sea in Mumbai?
questions.explanation: The Gateway of India in Mumbai is a large arch-monument built on the waterfront. It overlooks the Arabian Sea.
questions.funFact: You can take a boat ride from the Gateway of India to visit the famous Elephanta Caves on a nearby island!

questions.question: The Taj Mahal was built by which Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Emperor Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal between 1632 and 1653 in memory of his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal.
questions.funFact: More than 20,000 workers helped build the Taj Mahal, and it took about 22 years to finish!

questions.question: What does India Gate honour?
questions.explanation: India Gate is a war memorial dedicated to the 70,000 Indian soldiers who lost their lives fighting in World War I.
questions.funFact: There is an eternal flame called 'Amar Jawan Jyoti' near India Gate that burns day and night to remember brave soldiers!

questions.question: Which of these monuments is in Jaipur?
questions.explanation: Hawa Mahal, also known as the Palace of Winds, is located in Jaipur, Rajasthan. It was built in 1799.
questions.funFact: Hawa Mahal has 953 tiny windows! They were designed so that royal ladies could watch street festivals without being seen.

questions.question: The Golden Temple is a famous place of worship for which religion?
questions.explanation: The Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib) in Amritsar, Punjab, is the holiest shrine in Sikhism.
questions.funFact: The Golden Temple's kitchen (called Langar) feeds over 100,000 people every single day for free!

questions.question: How many minarets (towers) does the Charminar have?
questions.explanation: Charminar means 'Four Minarets' in Urdu. It has four grand arches and four minarets, one on each corner.
questions.funFact: Charminar was built in 1591 to celebrate the end of a terrible plague in the city of Hyderabad!

questions.question: Which monument is also called the 'Palace of Winds'?
questions.explanation: Hawa Mahal in Jaipur is called the Palace of Winds because its many windows allow cool breeze to flow through the building.
questions.funFact: Hawa Mahal looks like a giant honeycomb from the outside because of its unique shape with so many small windows!

questions.question: The Qutub Minar is located in which city?
questions.explanation: The Qutub Minar is located in the Mehrauli area of Delhi. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
questions.funFact: There is a mysterious iron pillar near the Qutub Minar that has not rusted for over 1,600 years — nobody knows exactly why!

questions.question: Which material was used to build the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is built mainly from white marble that was transported from Makrana in Rajasthan.
questions.funFact: Precious stones like jade, crystal, turquoise, and sapphire were used to decorate the walls of the Taj Mahal!

questions.question: Which of these cities is known as the 'Pink City'?
questions.explanation: Jaipur is called the Pink City because in 1876, the entire city was painted pink to welcome the Prince of Wales.
questions.funFact: Jaipur was one of India's first planned cities — its streets were designed in a grid pattern, like a chessboard!

questions.question: What body of water can you see from the Gateway of India?
questions.explanation: The Gateway of India faces the Arabian Sea. It is located at the tip of Apollo Bunder in South Mumbai.
questions.funFact: The Gateway of India was the last place the British troops passed through when they left India in 1948!

questions.question: Which famous monument has a beautiful garden with fountains in front of it?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal has a stunning Mughal garden divided into four parts with water channels and fountains leading up to it.
questions.funFact: The garden of the Taj Mahal is designed to look like the gardens of paradise described in ancient stories!

questions.question: The Victoria Memorial is a large white building in which city?
questions.explanation: The Victoria Memorial is located in Kolkata, West Bengal. It was built between 1906 and 1921 in memory of Queen Victoria.
questions.funFact: The Victoria Memorial has a revolving angel statue on top of its dome that turns with the wind!

questions.question: Who built the Hawa Mahal in Jaipur?
questions.explanation: Hawa Mahal was built in 1799 by Maharaja Sawai Pratap Singh of the Kachhwaha Rajput dynasty.
questions.funFact: Hawa Mahal has no stairs! The upper floors are reached using special ramps so that even royal palanquins could be carried up.

questions.question: The Mysore Palace is located in which state?
questions.explanation: The Mysore Palace is located in Mysore (Mysuru), Karnataka. It was the official residence of the Wodeyar dynasty.
questions.funFact: During the Dasara festival, the Mysore Palace is lit up with nearly 100,000 light bulbs — it looks like a palace from a fairy tale!

questions.question: The Konark Sun Temple in Odisha is shaped like a giant what?
questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple is designed in the shape of a gigantic chariot with 24 elaborately carved stone wheels pulled by seven horses.
questions.funFact: The 24 wheels of the Konark Sun Temple actually work as sundials — you can tell the exact time of day using the shadow they cast!

questions.question: Charminar is a famous monument in which city?
questions.explanation: Charminar is located in Hyderabad, Telangana. It was built in 1591 by Sultan Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah.
questions.funFact: There is a mosque on the top floor of the Charminar — it is one of the oldest mosques in Hyderabad!

questions.question: The Sanchi Stupa was built to honour which religious figure?
questions.explanation: The Sanchi Stupa in Madhya Pradesh was built by Emperor Ashoka in the 3rd century BCE to preserve and spread the teachings of Lord Buddha.
questions.funFact: The lion capital found at Sanchi inspired the national emblem of India that you can see on every Indian rupee coin!

questions.question: In which city is the Golden Temple (Harmandir Sahib) located?
questions.explanation: The Golden Temple is located in Amritsar, Punjab. Its construction was initiated by Guru Ram Das, the fourth Sikh Guru, in 1577.
questions.funFact: The upper part of the Golden Temple is covered with approximately 750 kg of pure gold!

questions.question: Who commissioned the building of Fatehpur Sikri?
questions.explanation: Fatehpur Sikri was built by Mughal Emperor Akbar in 1571 as his new capital. It is located near Agra, Uttar Pradesh.
questions.funFact: Fatehpur Sikri was abandoned just 14 years after it was built because the city ran out of water!

questions.question: The Meenakshi Temple is a famous Hindu temple in which city?
questions.explanation: The Meenakshi Amman Temple is located in Madurai, Tamil Nadu. It is dedicated to Goddess Meenakshi and Lord Sundareswarar.
questions.funFact: The Meenakshi Temple has about 33,000 sculptures! Its colourful towers (gopurams) are covered with thousands of painted figures of gods and animals.

questions.question: Which emperor built the Red Fort in Delhi?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort was built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in 1639 when he moved his capital from Agra to Delhi.
questions.funFact: The Red Fort once had a stream called 'Nahr-i-Bihisht' (Stream of Paradise) flowing through its royal apartments!

questions.question: The Ajanta Caves are famous for their ancient paintings and are located in which state?
questions.explanation: The Ajanta Caves are located in Aurangabad district, Maharashtra. They contain Buddhist paintings and sculptures dating from the 2nd century BCE.
questions.funFact: The Ajanta Caves were lost in the jungle for hundreds of years and were accidentally discovered by a British officer while hunting a tiger in 1819!

questions.question: The Brihadeeswarar Temple in Thanjavur was built by which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Brihadeeswarar Temple was built by Raja Raja Chola I of the Chola dynasty around 1010 CE in Thanjavur, Tamil Nadu.
questions.funFact: The temple's shadow never falls on the ground at noon! Its massive dome weighs about 80 tonnes and was placed on top without any modern machinery.

questions.question: Which of these monuments is located in Odisha?
questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple is located in Konark, Odisha. It was built in the 13th century by King Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Ganga dynasty.
questions.funFact: Sailors used to call the Konark Sun Temple the 'Black Pagoda' because it was so tall it could be seen from the sea and helped them navigate!

questions.question: Hampi, a famous ruins site, was once the capital of which empire?
questions.explanation: Hampi in Karnataka was the capital of the Vijayanagara Empire, one of the richest and largest Hindu empires in Indian history.
questions.funFact: At its peak, Hampi was one of the largest cities in the world — even bigger than Rome — with a population of about 500,000 people!

questions.question: The Ellora Caves contain temples of how many religions?
questions.explanation: The Ellora Caves in Maharashtra contain temples of three religions: Buddhism, Hinduism, and Jainism, showing the religious harmony of ancient India.
questions.funFact: The Kailasa Temple at Ellora was carved from a single rock from top to bottom — workers removed about 200,000 tonnes of rock to create it!

questions.question: Which monument was built to celebrate the end of a plague in Hyderabad?
questions.explanation: Sultan Muhammad Quli Qutb Shah built the Charminar in 1591 to thank God after a devastating plague ended in Hyderabad.
questions.funFact: The area around Charminar is famous for its bustling bazaars where you can buy beautiful bangles, pearls, and delicious Hyderabadi biryani!

questions.question: The Victoria Memorial in Kolkata is built in which architectural style?
questions.explanation: The Victoria Memorial is built in the Indo-Saracenic style, which blends British and Indian (Mughal) architectural elements.
questions.funFact: The Victoria Memorial now serves as a museum with 28,394 items including rare paintings, books, and even Queen Victoria's writing desk!

questions.question: Which ancient Indian university's ruins can be found in Bihar?
questions.explanation: The ruins of Nalanda University are in Bihar. It was one of the world's first residential universities, flourishing from the 5th to 12th century CE.
questions.funFact: Nalanda's library was so huge that it is said to have burned for three months when it was destroyed by invaders!

questions.question: The Lotus Temple in Delhi is a place of worship for which faith?
questions.explanation: The Lotus Temple in Delhi is a Bahai House of Worship. It is open to people of all religions and was completed in 1986.
questions.funFact: The Lotus Temple is made of 27 free-standing marble petals and uses no straight lines in its design — everything is curved!

questions.question: Which of these Indian monuments is NOT a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: While Mysore Palace is a popular tourist attraction, it is not a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The Taj Mahal, Qutub Minar, and Hampi are all UNESCO sites.
questions.funFact: India has 42 UNESCO World Heritage Sites as of 2024, making it one of the countries with the most heritage sites in the world!

questions.question: The Khajuraho temples are famous for their sculptures and belong to which period?
questions.explanation: The Khajuraho temples in Madhya Pradesh were built by the Chandela dynasty between 950 and 1050 CE, known for their stunning sculptural art.
questions.funFact: Out of the 85 temples originally built at Khajuraho, only 25 survive today. They were hidden by dense forests for centuries before being rediscovered!

questions.question: Which architectural style is the Brihadeeswarar Temple an example of?
questions.explanation: The Brihadeeswarar Temple is a masterpiece of Dravidian architecture, characterised by its towering vimana (temple tower) and elaborate gopurams (gateways).
questions.funFact: The temple's vimana rises to 66 metres, and the capstone at the top is a single granite block weighing about 80 tonnes — engineers still debate how it was lifted!

questions.question: In which year did the Taj Mahal become a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1983. It is described as 'the jewel of Muslim art in India.'
questions.funFact: The four minarets of the Taj Mahal are slightly tilted outward so that if they ever fell, they would fall away from the main tomb and not damage it!

questions.question: The rock-cut temples of Mahabalipuram were built by which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Group of Monuments at Mahabalipuram in Tamil Nadu were carved during the Pallava dynasty in the 7th and 8th centuries CE.
questions.funFact: Mahabalipuram has a giant boulder called 'Krishna's Butter Ball' that balances on a slope and has not moved for over 1,200 years despite many attempts to push it!

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor banned entry to the Qutub Minar after a stampede accident?
options: There was no ban by a Mughal emperor
questions.explanation: The ban on public entry inside the Qutub Minar was imposed by the government in 1981 after a tragic stampede, not by any Mughal emperor.
questions.funFact: The Qutub Minar has five distinct storeys, and each storey is decorated with a different pattern — you can see the style change as you look upward!

questions.question: The Konark Sun Temple was built by King Narasimhadeva I in which century?
questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple was built around 1250 CE (13th century) by King Narasimhadeva I of the Eastern Ganga dynasty in Odisha.
questions.funFact: The temple originally had a massive magnet at the top that is said to have kept the main idol floating in mid-air due to magnetic force!

questions.question: Which of these monuments was built using the pietra dura technique of inlaid gemstones?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal features pietra dura, an Italian technique where semi-precious stones are inlaid into marble to create intricate floral and geometric patterns.
questions.funFact: Over 28 types of precious and semi-precious stones were sourced from across Asia to decorate the Taj Mahal, including lapis lazuli from Afghanistan and turquoise from Tibet!

questions.question: The Hampi ruins were destroyed in 1565 after which battle?
questions.explanation: The Vijayanagara Empire's capital at Hampi was devastated after the Battle of Talikota in 1565, when a coalition of Deccan sultanates defeated the empire.
questions.funFact: Hampi's ruins spread across 26 square kilometres — it is so vast that archaeologists are still discovering new structures hidden underground!

questions.question: The Sanchi Stupa's famous toranas (gateways) depict scenes from which texts?
questions.explanation: The carved gateways of the Sanchi Stupa depict scenes from the Jataka Tales, which are stories about the previous lives of Lord Buddha.
questions.funFact: Interestingly, the earliest carvings at Sanchi never show Buddha in human form — instead, he is represented by symbols like a footprint, a tree, or an empty throne!

questions.question: Which monument complex in Rajasthan includes Jantar Mantar, an astronomical observation site?
questions.explanation: Jantar Mantar is an astronomical observation site in Jaipur, built by Maharaja Sawai Jai Singh II in 1734. It is part of the Jaipur City UNESCO site.
questions.funFact: Jantar Mantar in Jaipur has the world's largest stone sundial called Samrat Yantra — it is 27 metres tall and can tell time accurate to within 2 seconds!

questions.question: Which of these pairs correctly matches a monument with its primary building material?
questions.explanation: The Konark Sun Temple is built primarily from khondalite stone, a type of rock found in Odisha. Hawa Mahal uses red and pink sandstone, Red Fort uses red sandstone, and Mysore Palace uses granite with carved wooden doors.
questions.funFact: Khondalite stone gets its name from the Khond tribe of Odisha, and the same type of stone was also used to build the Jagannath Temple in Puri!
